    The supervisor had extra leisure time, the gardens were freely visited.
    The lowly minister wore a hairpin and pen, supporting the holy one with harmonious music.
    Walking out of the palace at night, it was cool; precious trees were bathed in clear dew.
    The celestial phenomena are indeed splendid, resounding like jade and stone.
    Being commanded to compose a harmonious piece, looking back, it was difficult to complete.
    My weak nature could not sustain itself, it was fragile, decaying, and withered.
    Who would have thought for over a decade, the tombs were cold, with paulownia and Chinese parasol trees.
    Today, in the country of K'un-ming, again, wearing the official cap.
    Serving the emperor in the distant palace, climbing high into the clouds.
    But recalling the time at the Western Garden, for a moment, sorrow and bitterness filled the heart. [51]

    君昔汉公卿，未央冠群贤。
    　　倘若念平生，览此同怆然。
    　　其余七篇，传者失本。
    　　王粲谓总曰：“吾本短小，无何取乐进女，女似其父，短小尤甚。自别君后， 改娶刘荆州女。寻生一子，荆州与名似翁奴，今年十八，长七尺三寸，所恨未得 参丈人也。当渠年十一，与余同览镜，余谓之曰：‘汝首魁梧于余。’渠立应余 曰：‘防风骨节专车，不如白起头小而锐。’余又谓曰：‘汝长大当为将。’又 应余曰：‘仲尼三尺童子，羞言霸道。况某承大人严训，敢措意于相斫道乎？’ 余知其了了过人矣。不知足下生来有郎娘否？”良久沉思，稍如相识，因曰： “二君子既是总友人，何计可脱小吏之厄？”徐幹曰：“君但执前集，诉于县宰， 则脱矣。”总又问：“坤明是何国？”幹曰：“魏开国邺地也。公昔为开国侍中， 何遽忘也？”公在坤明国家累悉无恙，贤小娘子娇羞娘，有一篇奉忆，昨者已诵 似丈人矣，诗曰： [52]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [52]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 冠群贤 - Denotes being the best among many.
    • 怆然 - Conveys a sense of deep sorrow or melancholy.
    • 失本 - Means losing the original meaning or source.
    • 了了过人 - Expresses being exceptionally intelligent.
    • 相斫道 - Refers to methods of mutual killing or warfare.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 汉公卿 - Refers to high-ranking officials in the Han dynasty.
    • 未央 - The imperial palace of the Han dynasty, symbolizing the pinnacle of power and prestige.
    • 乐进 - A general in the Three Kingdoms period known for his bravery.
    • 刘荆州 - Liu Biao, the governor of Jing Province during the late Eastern Han and early Three Kingdoms periods.
    • 防风 - A mythical giant in Chinese mythology.
    • 白起 - A famous general from the Warring States period.
    • 仲尼 - Confucius, the great philosopher and teacher.
    • 霸道 - A term used to describe tyrannical or aggressive rule, often contrasted with '王道' (virtuous rule).
    • 总 - Refers to a person named Zong, a friend of the speaker.
    • 小吏 - A minor official, often used to describe someone in a low-ranking position.
    • 坤明 - The capital of the state, possibly referring to the Wei dynasty's capital, Luoyang.
    • 娇羞娘 - A term of endearment for a young woman, possibly Xiao Xiuniang.

    Proper Nouns

    • 君 - The addressee, a high-ranking official in the Han dynasty.
    • 乐进 - A general in the Three Kingdoms period.
    • 刘荆州 - Liu Biao, the governor of Jing Province.
    • 防风 - A mythical giant in Chinese mythology.
    • 白起 - A famous general from the Warring States period.
    • 仲尼 - Confucius.
    • 总 - A person named Zong, a friend of the speaker.
    • 小吏 - A minor official.
    • 坤明 - The capital of the state, possibly referring to the Wei dynasty's capital, Luoyang.
    • 娇羞娘 - Xiao Xiuniang, a young woman.

    You were once a high official in Han, ranking among the foremost among the multitude of talented men.
    If you recall your lifetime, seeing this will make us both sorrowful.
    The other seven pieces, their transmission has lost the original meaning.
    Wang Can said to Zong, "I was originally short, and why did I take the daughter of Le Jin? She resembles her father, even more so in her shortness. After parting from you, I remarried the daughter of Liu Jingzhou. Soon after, I had a son, named by Liu Jingzhou like a servant, now eighteen years old, seven feet three inches tall. What I regret is not being able to meet my father-in-law. When he was eleven, he and I looked at the mirror together. I told him, 'Your head is more imposing than mine.' He immediately replied, 'Fangfeng's bones are so large they require a cart, but it's not as good as Bai Qi's small yet sharp head.' I then told him, 'You will grow up to be a general.' He responded, 'Confucius, even as a three-foot-tall child, would be ashamed to speak of tyrannical rule. Especially since I have been taught by my elders, how dare I think about the ways of mutual slaughter?' I knew he was exceptionally intelligent. I wonder if you have any sons or daughters since then?" After a long period of deep thought, he slowly seemed to recognize someone, and said, "Since the two gentlemen are friends of Zong, what plan can save the small official from his plight?" Xu Gan said, "You just need to present your previous collection and appeal to the county magistrate, and you will be saved." Zong asked again, "What is the country of Kunming?" Gan replied, "It is the capital of the Wei dynasty, located in Ye." You were once a Grand Attendant in the founding state, how could you forget so soon? You and your family in Kunming are all well, the virtuous young lady, Xiao Xiuniang, has a piece dedicated to you, which I recited to your father-in-law yesterday. The poem says: [52]

    忆爷抛女不归家，不作侍中为小吏。
    　　就辛苦，弃荣华，愿爷相念早相见，
    　　与儿买李市甘瓜。
    　　诵讫，总不觉涕泪交下，为一章寄娇羞娘子：
    　　忆儿貌，念儿心，望儿不见泪沾襟。
    　　时殊世异难相见，弃谢此生当访寻。
    　　既而王粲、徐幹与总殷勤叙别。
    　　乃携《刘桢集》五卷，并具陈见王粲、徐幹之状，仍说前生是刘桢。县宰因 见桢卒后诗，大惊曰：“不可使刘公干为小吏。”即解遣，以宾礼待之。后不知 总所在，集亦寻失矣。时人勖子弟皆曰：“死刘桢犹庇得生顾总，可不进修哉！” [53]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [53]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 憶爺抛女不归家 - Literal phrase meaning 'Remembering Father abandoned Daughter and did not return home,' conveying a sense of abandonment and longing.
    • 不作侍中为小吏 - Literally 'Did not become a Shizhong but a minor official,' indicating a decline in status or position.
    • 就辛苦，弃荣华 - Means 'Endured hardships, abandoned glory,' suggesting a shift from luxury to hardship.
    • 愿爷相念早相见 - Literally 'Wish Father remembers and meets early,' expressing a desire for reunion.
    • 与儿买李市甘瓜 - Means 'With me, buy plums and sweet melons,' implying a simple, shared activity.
    • 诵讫，总不觉涕泪交下 - Means 'After reciting, suddenly couldn't help but cry,' indicating an emotional response.
    • 为一章寄娇羞娘子 - Means 'Wrote a chapter to send to the shy and delicate lady,' suggesting a poetic or literary composition.
    • 忆儿貌，念儿心 - Means 'Remembering Daughter's appearance, thinking of Daughter's heart,' emphasizing emotional connection.
    • 望儿不见泪沾襟 - Means 'Looking at Daughter but not seeing, tears wet the collar,' expressing sorrow and longing.
    • 时殊世异难相见 - Means 'Times are different, world is changed, hard to meet,' indicating the difficulty of reunion due to changing circumstances.
    • 弃谢此生当访寻 - Means 'Abandoned and thanked this life, should seek out,' suggesting a resolution to find each other again.
    • 既而王粲、徐幹与总殷勤叙别 - Means 'Later, Wang Can and Xu Gan were kind and bid farewell to Zong,' indicating a formal farewell.
    • 乃携《刘桢集》五卷 - Means 'Then carried the five volumes of Liu Zhen's collection,' suggesting a physical act of carrying books.
    • 并具陈见王粲、徐幹之状 - Means 'And detailed the encounter with Wang Can and Xu Gan,' indicating a recounting of events.
    • 仍说前生是刘桢 - Means 'Also said the previous life was Liu Zhen,' suggesting a spiritual or reincarnation belief.
    • 县宰因见桢卒后诗 - Means 'The county magistrate saw the poems after Liu Zhen's death,' indicating a posthumous recognition.
    • 不可使刘公干为小吏 - Means 'Cannot let Liu Gonggan be a minor official,' indicating a decision to elevate his status.
    • 即解遣，以宾礼待之 - Means 'Immediately released, treated with courtesy,' indicating a change in treatment.
    • 后不知总所在，集亦寻失矣 - Means 'Later, did not know where Zong was, the collection was also lost,' indicating a loss of both person and work.
    • 时人勖子弟皆曰 - Means 'People at the time encouraged their children, all said,' indicating a general moral lesson.
    • 死刘桢犹庇得生顾总 - Means 'Even dead Liu Zhen still protected living Gu Zong,' suggesting a metaphorical or spiritual protection.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 刘桢 - A famous poet of the Three Kingdoms period, known for his literary works.
    • 王粲 - A renowned poet and scholar of the Three Kingdoms period, known for his literary and political contributions.
    • 徐幹 - A poet and literary figure of the Three Kingdoms period, known for his contributions to the Jian'an literary movement.
    • 县宰 - Refers to the magistrate or county official, indicating a local government figure.
    • 刘公干 - A title or nickname for Liu Zhen, emphasizing his status as a respected literary figure.
    • 宾礼 - Refers to the courtesy and respect shown to guests or distinguished individuals.
    • 时人勖子弟 - Indicates a general moral lesson or advice given to the younger generation.

    Proper Nouns

    • 刘桢 - A famous poet of the Three Kingdoms period.
    • 王粲 - A renowned poet and scholar of the Three Kingdoms period.
    • 徐幹 - A poet and literary figure of the Three Kingdoms period.
    • 县宰 - The magistrate or county official.
    • 刘公干 - A title or nickname for Liu Zhen.
    • 宾礼 - The courtesy and respect shown to guests or distinguished individuals.
    • 时人勖子弟 - People at the time encouraging their children.

    Remembering Father abandoned Daughter and did not return home, he did not become a Shizhong but a minor official.
    He endured hardships, abandoned glory, and wished Father would remember and meet early,
    with me, buy plums and sweet melons.
    After reciting, he suddenly couldn't help but cry, and wrote a chapter to send to the shy and delicate lady:
    Remembering Daughter's appearance, thinking of Daughter's heart, looking at Daughter but not seeing, tears wet the collar.
    Times are different, world is changed, hard to meet; abandoned and thanked this life, should seek out.
    Later, Wang Can and Xu Gan were kind and bid farewell to Zong.
    Then carried the five volumes of Liu Zhen's collection, and detailed the encounter with Wang Can and Xu Gan, also saying the previous life was Liu Zhen. The county magistrate saw the poems after Liu Zhen's death, and was greatly surprised, saying, "Cannot let Liu Gonggan be a minor official." Immediately released, treated with courtesy. Later, did not know where Zong was, the collection was also lost. People at the time encouraged their children, all said, "Even dead Liu Zhen still protected living Gu Zong, how can one not strive to improve!" [53]

    ○周静帝居延部落主
    　　周静帝初，居延部落主勃都骨低凌暴，奢逸好乐，居处甚盛。忽有人数十至 门，一人先投剌曰：“省名部落主成多受。”因趋入。骨低问曰：“何故省名部 落？”多受曰：“某等数人各殊，名字皆不别造。有姓马者，姓皮者，姓鹿者， 姓熊者，姓獐者，姓卫者，姓班者，然皆名受。唯某帅名多受耳。”骨低曰： “君等悉似伶官，有何所解？”多受曰：“晓弄碗珠。性不爱俗，言皆经义。” 骨低大喜曰：“目所未睹。”有一优即前曰：“某等肚饥，臈臈怡怡，皮漫 绕身三匝。主人食若不充，开口终当不舍。”骨低悦，更命加食。一人曰：“某 请弄大小相成，终始相生。”于是长人吞短人，肥人吞瘦人，相吞残两人。长者 又曰：“请作终始相生耳。”于是吐下一人，吐者又吐一人。递相吐出，人数复 足。骨低甚惊，因重赐赍遣之。 [54]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [54]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 居延部落主 - Refers to the leader of the Juyan tribe, a historical tribal leader in the region of Juyan, which was part of the Northern Wei and later the Western Wei and Northern Zhou dynasties.
    • 凌暴 - Means 'to bully' or 'to oppress.'
    • 奢逸好乐 - Means 'extravagant and fond of pleasure.'
    • 居处甚盛 - Means 'living in a very grand manner.'
    • 投剌 - An ancient custom where a person would present a card or letter to introduce themselves.
    • 省名部落 - Means 'to name the tribe.'
    • 各殊 - Means 'each unique.'
    • 名字皆不别造 - Means 'all names are not specially created.'
    • 姓马者，姓皮者，姓鹿者，姓熊者，姓獐者，姓卫者，姓班者 - Refers to people with surnames like Ma, Pi, Lu, Xiong, Zhang, Wei, and Ban.
    • 然皆名受 - Means 'but all are named Shou.'
    • 唯某帅名多受耳 - Means 'only my leader's name is Duo Shou.'
    • 悉似伶官 - Means 'all seem like court jesters.'
    • 有何所解 - Means 'what do you understand?'
    • 晓弄碗珠 - Means 'skilled in playing with bowls and pearls,' possibly referring to a form of entertainment or acrobatics.
    • 性不爱俗 - Means 'nature does not love the common.'
    • 言皆经义 - Means 'words are all based on classical principles.'
    • 目所未睹 - Means 'something I have never seen before.'
    • 臈臈怡怡 - Means 'happy and content.'
    • 皮漫绕身三匝 - Means 'skin wrapped around the body three times.'
    • 主人食若不充 - Means 'if the host's food is not sufficient.'
    • 开口终当不舍 - Means 'will not stop opening the mouth.'
    • 弄大小相成，终始相生 - Means 'to play with the big and small, the beginning and the end.'
    • 长人吞短人，肥人吞瘦人 - Means 'tall people swallowing short people, fat people swallowing thin people.'
    • 递相吐出 - Means 'one after another spitting out.'
    • 人数复足 - Means 'the number of people is restored.'
    • 重赐赍遣之 - Means 'to heavily reward and send them away.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 周静帝 - Refers to Emperor Jing of Northern Zhou, the last emperor of the Northern Zhou dynasty.
    • 居延 - Refers to the Juyan region, a historical area in the northwest of China.
    • 部落主 - Refers to the leader of a tribal group, a common title in the nomadic and semi-nomadic societies of the time.
    • 伶官 - Refers to court jesters or entertainers, often skilled in various forms of performance.
    • 碗珠 - Refers to a form of entertainment or acrobatics involving bowls and pearls.

    Proper Nouns

    • 周静帝 - The reign title of Emperor Jing of Northern Zhou.
    • 居延部落主 - The title of the leader of the Juyan tribe.
    • 勃都骨低 - The name of the tribal leader.
    • 成多受 - The name of the person who introduced himself.

    During the reign of Emperor Jing of Northern Zhou, the tribal leader of Juyan, Bo Du Gu Di, was known for his oppressive behavior, extravagant lifestyle, and love for pleasure. He lived in great splendor. Suddenly, several people arrived at his door, and one of them first presented a card, saying, “I am the leader of the tribe, Cheng Duo Shou.” He then hurriedly entered. Bo Du Gu Di asked, “Why did you name the tribe?” Cheng Duo Shou replied, “We several people are all unique, and our names are not specially created. There are those surnamed Ma, Pi, Lu, Xiong, Zhang, Wei, and Ban, but all of us are named Shou. Only my leader’s name is Duo Shou.” Bo Du Gu Di said, “You all seem like court jesters, what do you understand?” Cheng Duo Shou replied, “We are skilled in playing with bowls and pearls. We do not love the common, and our words are all based on classical principles.” Bo Du Gu Di was greatly delighted and said, “I have never seen anything like this before.” One of the jesters stepped forward and said, “We are all hungry, happy and content, with our skin wrapped around our bodies three times. If the host’s food is not sufficient, we will not stop opening our mouths.” Bo Du Gu Di was pleased and ordered more food to be brought. One of them said, “I will perform a trick of the big and small, the beginning and the end.” He then had tall people swallow short people, and fat people swallow thin people, until two people were left. The taller one then said, “Please perform the beginning and the end.” He then spat out one person, and the one who spat out another person. One after another, they spat out, and the number of people was restored. Bo Du Gu Di was greatly surprised and heavily rewarded and sent them away. [54]

    明日又至，戏弄如初。连翩半月，骨低颇烦，不能设食。诸伶皆怒曰：“主 人当以某等为幻术，请借郎君娘子试之。”于是持骨低儿女弟妹甥侄妻妾等吞之 于腹中。腹中皆啼呼请命，骨低惶怖，降阶顿首，哀乞亲属。伶者皆笑曰：“此 无伤，不足忧。”即吐出之，亲属完全如初。 [55]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [55]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 戲弄如初 - Indicates the continuation of previous behavior.
    • 連翩半月 - Describes a prolonged period, but the exact duration is not specified.
    • 吞之於腹中 - A metaphorical expression indicating the musicians' ability to perform illusions.
    • 完全如初 - Describes the state of the individuals after being expelled, emphasizing the miraculous nature of the event.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 骨低 - A proper name, likely a character in the story.
    • 郎君娘子 - Refers to the son and daughter of Bone Low.
    • 伶者 - Refers to musicians or performers, often with a connotation of skill and entertainment.

    Proper Nouns

    • 骨低 - A proper name, likely a character in the story.
    • 郎君娘子 - Refers to the son and daughter of Bone Low.
    • 伶者 - Refers to musicians or performers, often with a connotation of skill and entertainment.

    The next day, he came again and played tricks as before. For half a month in succession, Bone Low was quite troubled and could not prepare food. All the musicians were angry and said, "Master, we should use you and your family as subjects for our illusions. Please let us borrow your son and daughter to try it out." They then swallowed Bone Low's children, nieces, nephews, and other relatives into their stomachs. Those inside the stomachs all cried out for mercy. Bone Low, terrified, descended from the steps and bowed, appealing pitifully for his family. The musicians all laughed and said, "There is no need to worry; this is harmless." They then expelled the relatives, who were completely as before. [55]

    骨低深怒，欲伺隙杀之。因令密访之。见至一古宅基而灭。骨低闻而令掘之， 深数尺，于瓦砾下得一大木槛。中有皮袋数千。槛旁有谷麦，触即为灰。槛中得 竹简书，文字磨灭，不可识。唯隐隐似有三数字，若是“陵”字。骨低知是诸袋 为怪，欲举出焚之。诸袋因号呼槛中曰：“某等无命，寻合化灭。缘李都尉留水 银在此，故得且存。某等即都尉李少卿般粮袋，屋崩平压，绵历岁月，今已有命， 见为居延山神收作伶人，伏乞存情于神，不相残毁。自此不敢复扰高居矣。”骨 低利其水银，尽焚诸袋。无不为冤楚声，血流漂洒。焚讫，骨低房廊户牖悉为冤 痛之音，如焚袋时，月余日不止。其年骨低举家病死，死者相继，周岁无复孑遗。 水银后亦失所在。 [56]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [56]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 骨低深怒 - Compound name '骨低' combined with emotional state '深怒,' meaning 'Bone Low' was deeply angry.
    • 伺隙 - Means 'to wait for an opportunity' or 'to look for a chance.'
    • 隐隐似有三数字 - Means 'faintly resembling three characters.'
    • 居延山神 - Refers to the god of the Juanyuan Mountain.
    • 冤楚声 - Means 'sounds of suffering and lamentation.'
    • 冤痛之音 - Means 'sounds of sorrow and pain.'
    • 相继 - Means 'one after another.'
    • 孑遗 - Means 'survivors' or 'remnants.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 骨低 - A name, possibly a historical figure or a fictional character.
    • 李都尉 - Refers to a military officer with the title of '都尉' (commander).
    • 李少卿 - Refers to a person with the title of '少卿' (a high-ranking official).
    • 居延山 - Refers to the Juanyuan Mountain, a specific location in Chinese history.
    • 水银 - Mercury, a chemical element often used in alchemy and medicine.
    • 屋崩平压 - Refers to a house collapsing and crushing something.
    • 绵历岁月 - Means 'over many years.'
    • 伏乞存情于神 - Means 'to request the god's compassion.'

    Proper Nouns

    • 骨低 - A name, possibly a historical figure or a fictional character.
    • 李都尉 - A military officer with the title of '都尉.'
    • 李少卿 - A high-ranking official with the title of '少卿.'
    • 居延山 - The Juanyuan Mountain.
    • 水银 - Mercury.

    Bone Low was deeply enraged and sought an opportunity to kill him. He then ordered a secret investigation. They discovered that the person had disappeared at the site of an ancient house foundation. Upon hearing this, Bone Low ordered the site to be excavated. After digging several feet deep, they found a large wooden cage beneath the broken tiles and debris. Inside the cage were thousands of leather bags. Beside the cage was some grain and wheat, which turned to ash upon contact. In the cage, they found bamboo slips with faded and unreadable text, except for three faint characters that resembled the word "陵" (mound). Bone Low realized that the bags were supernatural and intended to burn them. The bags cried out from within the cage, saying, "We are doomed and will soon be destroyed. We have been preserved here because Commander Li left mercury with us. We are the grain bags of Commander Li Shaoqing. The house collapsed and crushed us, and over many years, we have now been granted a new life, taken by the god of Juanyuan Mountain to become entertainers. We beg the god to have mercy and not to destroy us. From now on, we will not disturb the high and noble." Bone Low coveted the mercury and burned all the bags. The bags emitted sounds of suffering and lamentation, and blood flowed and splattered. After the burning, the halls, corridors, doors, and windows of Bone Low's house were filled with sounds of sorrow and pain, just as they had been when the bags were burned, for more than a month without stopping. That year, Bone Low's entire family fell ill and died, one after another, with no survivors remaining after a year. The mercury also disappeared. [56]

    ○刘讽
    　　文明年，竟陵掾刘讽，夜投夷陵空馆，月明下憩。忽有一女郎西轩至，仪质 温丽，缓歌闲步，徐徐至中轩，回命青衣曰：“紫绥，取西堂花茵来，兼屈刘家 六姨姨、十四舅母、南邻翘翘小娘子，并将溢奴来，传语道此间好风月，足得游 乐。弹琴咏诗，大是好事。虽有竟陵判司，此人已睡明月下，不足回避也。”
    　　未几而三女郎至，一孩儿，色皆绝国。于是紫绥铺花茵于庭中，揖让班坐。 坐中设犀角酒樽，象牙杓，绿罽花觯，白琉璃盏，醪醴馨香，远闻空际。女郎谈 谑歌咏，音词清婉。一女郎为明府，一女郎为录事，明府女郎举觞浇酒曰：“愿 三姨婆寿等祇果山，六姨姨与三姨婆寿等，刘姨夫得太山府纠判官，翘翘小娘子 嫁得诸余国太子，溢奴便作诸余国宰相，某三四女伴总嫁得地府司文舍人，不然， 嫁得平等王郎君六郎子、七郎子，则平生素望足矣。”一时皆笑曰：“须与蔡家 娘子赏口。 [57]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [57]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 文明年 - Reign title of Emperor Wen of Tang (627-649 CE), indicating the historical context of the story.
    • 竟陵掾 - A local official in Jingling, indicating Duan Feng's official position.
    • 夷陵空馆 - An empty inn in Yiling, setting the scene for the encounter.
    • 缓歌闲步 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'singing softly and walking leisurely,' used to portray the grace and elegance of the female figure.
    • 徐徐至中轩 - 'Slowly arriving at the central pavilion,' describing the movement of the female figure.
    • 回命青衣 - 'Turned and ordered a maidservant,' indicating the command given to a servant.
    • 紫绥 - A name, possibly a servant or attendant.
    • 取西堂花茵来 - 'Fetch the flower mat from the west hall,' a specific instruction to bring a particular item.
    • 兼屈刘家六姨姨、十四舅母、南邻翘翘小娘子，并将溢奴来 - 'Also invite Aunt Liu Six, Aunt Uncle Fourteen, and Miss Qiaoqiao from the south neighbor, along with Ouyi the servant,' a list of people invited to join.
    • 传语道此间好风月，足得游乐 - 'Tell them that the wind and moon here are beautiful enough for enjoyment,' a message conveying the pleasant atmosphere.
    • 弹琴咏诗，大是好事 - 'Playing the lute and reciting poetry is a great thing,' expressing the joy of cultural activities.
    • 虽有竟陵判司，此人已睡明月下，不足回避也 - 'Even though there is a judge in Jingling, this person is already asleep under the bright moon, so there's no need to avoid him,' a statement indicating the absence of concern for the judge.
    • 未几而三女郎至 - 'Soon, three young ladies arrived,' describing the arrival of the invited guests.
    • 色皆绝国 - 'All of them were exceptionally beautiful,' emphasizing the attractiveness of the guests.
    • 于是紫绥铺花茵于庭中 - 'Then Zi Sui laid the flower mat in the courtyard,' describing the setup for the gathering.
    • 揖让班坐 - 'They bowed and took their seats,' indicating the formal seating arrangement.
    • 犀角酒樽，象牙杓，绿罽花觯，白琉璃盏 - 'Rhino horn wine vessel, ivory ladle, green carpet flower cup, white glass cup,' describing the luxurious and elegant tableware.
    • 醪醴馨香，远闻空际 - 'The fragrant wine could be smelled far away,' describing the pleasant aroma of the wine.
    • 女郎谈谑歌咏，音词清婉 - 'The young ladies chatted, sang, and recited poetry with clear and graceful voices,' describing the pleasant atmosphere of the gathering.
    • 明府女郎举觞浇酒 - 'The lady magistrate raised her cup to pour wine,' describing the act of toasting.
    • 愿三姨婆寿等祇果山，六姨姨与三姨婆寿等 - 'May Aunt Three live as long as Mount Zhi Guo, and Aunt Six live as long as Aunt Three,' expressing wishes for longevity.
    • 刘姨夫得太山府纠判官，翘翘小娘子嫁得诸余国太子，溢奴便作诸余国宰相 - 'May Aunt Liu's husband become a judge in Taishan Prefecture, and Miss Qiaoqiao marry the crown prince of Zhiyu Kingdom, and Ouyi become the prime minister of Zhiyu Kingdom,' expressing wishes for success and status.
    • 某三四女伴总嫁得地府司文舍人，不然，嫁得平等王郎君六郎子、七郎子 - 'May my three or four companions all become clerks in the Department of Literature in the underworld, or else marry the sixth and seventh sons of King Pingdeng,' expressing wishes for a good marriage.
    • 须与蔡家娘子赏口 - 'We must also share a toast with Madam Cai,' indicating the inclusion of another person in the toast.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 文明年 - The reign title of Emperor Wen of Tang, indicating the historical period.
    • 竟陵掾 - A local official in Jingling, reflecting the administrative structure of the Tang dynasty.
    • 夷陵 - A place name, indicating the location of the story.
    • 明府 - A respectful term for a magistrate or local official.
    • 录事 - A low-ranking official in charge of records and documents.
    • 太山府纠判官 - A high-ranking official in Taishan Prefecture, indicating a position of authority.
    • 诸余国太子 - The crown prince of Zhiyu Kingdom, indicating a royal figure.
    • 地府司文舍人 - Clerks in the Department of Literature in the underworld, reflecting the belief in an afterlife and bureaucratic structure.
    • 平等王郎君六郎子、七郎子 - The sixth and seventh sons of King Pingdeng, indicating noble figures.

    Proper Nouns

    • 文明年 - The reign title of Emperor Wen of Tang.
    • 竟陵 - A place name, indicating the location of the story.
    • 夷陵 - A place name, indicating the location of the story.
    • 明府 - A respectful term for a magistrate or local official.
    • 录事 - A low-ranking official in charge of records and documents.
    • 太山府纠判官 - A high-ranking official in Taishan Prefecture.
    • 诸余国太子 - The crown prince of Zhiyu Kingdom.
    • 地府司文舍人 - Clerks in the Department of Literature in the underworld.
    • 平等王郎君六郎子、七郎子 - The sixth and seventh sons of King Pingdeng.
    • 蔡家娘子 - A woman from the Cai family.

    In the year of Wenming, Liu Feng, an official of Jingling, spent the night at an empty inn in Yiling. Under the bright moonlight, he took a rest. Suddenly, a young lady appeared from the west pavilion, her demeanor graceful and her appearance beautiful. She sang softly and walked leisurely, slowly reaching the central pavilion. She turned and ordered a maidservant, saying, "Zi Sui, fetch the flower mat from the west hall, and also invite Aunt Liu Six, Aunt Uncle Fourteen, and Miss Qiaoqiao from the south neighbor, along with Ouyi the servant. Tell them that the wind and moon here are beautiful enough for enjoyment. Playing the lute and reciting poetry is a great thing. Even though there is a judge in Jingling, this person is already asleep under the bright moon, so there's no need to avoid him."

    Soon, three young ladies arrived, accompanied by a child, all exceptionally beautiful. Zi Sui then laid the flower mat in the courtyard, and they bowed and took their seats. At the table were placed a rhino horn wine vessel, an ivory ladle, a green carpet flower cup, and a white glass cup. The fragrant wine could be smelled far away. The young ladies chatted, sang, and recited poetry with clear and graceful voices. One of the young ladies was a magistrate, and another was a low-ranking official. The magistrate's daughter raised her cup to pour wine and said, "May Aunt Three live as long as Mount Zhi Guo, and Aunt Six live as long as Aunt Three. May Aunt Liu's husband become a judge in Taishan Prefecture, and Miss Qiaoqiao marry the crown prince of Zhiyu Kingdom, and Ouyi become the prime minister of Zhiyu Kingdom. May my three or four companions all become clerks in the Department of Literature in the underworld, or else marry the sixth and seventh sons of King Pingdeng. Then my lifelong aspirations would be fulfilled." They all laughed and said, "We must also share a toast with Madam Cai." [57]

    ”翘翘录事独下一筹，罚蔡家娘子曰：“刘姨夫才貌温茂，何故不与 他五道主使，空称纠判官，怕六姨姨不欢，深吃一盏。”蔡家娘子即持杯曰： “诚知被罚，直缘刘姨夫年老眼暗，恐看五道黄纸文书不得，误大神伯公事。饮 亦何伤。”于是众女郎皆笑倒。又一女郎起，传口令，仍抽一翠簪，急说，须传 翠簪，翠簪过令不通即罚。令曰：“鸾老头脑好，好头脑鸾老。”传说数巡，因 令紫绥下坐，使说令，紫绥素吃讷，令至，但称“鸾老鸾老”。女郎皆笑，曰： “昔贺若弼弄长孙鸾侍郎，以其年老口吃，又无发，故造此令。” [58]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [58]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 翘翘录事独下一筹 - Playful and ambiguous phrase; '翘翘' suggests something light or trivial, '录事' refers to a recorder, and '独下一筹' means to outdo others by a small margin, often used humorously.
    • 罚蔡家娘子曰 - '罚' means to punish or fine, and '娘子' is a term of respect for a woman, often used formally.
    • 刘姨夫才貌温茂 - '刘姨夫' refers to a man named Liu who is the husband of the aunt, '才貌温茂' means 'talented and handsome with a gentle and warm demeanor.'
    • 何故不与他五道主使 - '何故' means 'why,' '与' means 'to give,' '他' means 'him,' and '五道主使' is a specific role or title, possibly related to a ritual or administrative function.
    • 空称纠判官 - '空称' means 'to call oneself' or 'to pretend to be,' and '纠判官' is a title, possibly referring to a judge or official.
    • 怕六姨姨不欢 - '怕' means 'fear' or 'worry,' '六姨姨' is a term of respect for an aunt, and '不欢' means 'unhappy.'
    • 深吃一盏 - '深吃' means 'to drink deeply,' and '一盏' means 'a cup' or 'a bowl.'
    • 诚知被罚 - '诚知' means 'truly know,' and '被罚' means 'to be punished.'
    • 直缘刘姨夫年老眼暗 - '直缘' means 'because' or 'due to,' '年老' means 'old age,' and '眼暗' means 'dim-sighted' or 'weak eyesight.'
    • 恐看五道黄纸文书不得 - '恐' means 'fear' or 'worry,' '看' means 'to see,' and '五道黄纸文书' is a specific document or text, possibly related to a ritual or administrative function.
    • 误大神伯公事 - '误' means 'to miss' or 'to fail,' '大神伯' is a title, possibly referring to a deity or a high-ranking official, and '公事' means 'official business.'
    • 饮亦何伤 - '饮' means 'to drink,' and '何伤' means 'what harm.'
    • 鸾老头脑好 - '鸾老' is a term of respect for an elderly person, and '头脑好' means 'has a good mind' or 'is intelligent.'
    • 好头脑鸾老 - Playful repetition of the previous phrase, emphasizing the intelligence of the elderly person.
    • 昔贺若弼弄长孙鸾侍郎 - '昔' means 'in the past,' '贺若弼' is a historical figure, a general during the Sui Dynasty, '弄' means 'to play with' or 'to mock,' '长孙鸾' is a historical figure, a high-ranking official during the Tang Dynasty, and '侍郎' is a title for a high-ranking official.
    • 以其年老口吃 - '以其' means 'because,' '年老' means 'old age,' and '口吃' means 'stuttering.'
    • 又无发 - '又' means 'also,' and '无发' means 'no hair' or 'bald.'
    • 故造此令 - '故' means 'therefore,' and '造此令' means 'to create this command' or 'to invent this rule.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 蔡家娘子 - A woman from the Cai family, likely a fictional or historical figure in the context of the story.
    • 刘姨夫 - A man named Liu who is the husband of the aunt, likely a fictional or historical figure.
    • 五道主使 - A specific role or title, possibly related to a ritual or administrative function.
    • 纠判官 - A title, possibly referring to a judge or official.
    • 六姨姨 - A term of respect for an aunt.
    • 大神伯 - A title, possibly referring to a deity or a high-ranking official.
    • 贺若弼 - A historical figure, a general during the Sui Dynasty.
    • 长孙鸾 - A historical figure, a high-ranking official during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 侍郎 - A title for a high-ranking official.

    Proper Nouns

    • 蔡家娘子 - A woman from the Cai family.
    • 刘姨夫 - A man named Liu who is the husband of the aunt.
    • 五道主使 - A specific role or title.
    • 纠判官 - A title, possibly referring to a judge or official.
    • 六姨姨 - A term of respect for an aunt.
    • 大神伯 - A title, possibly referring to a deity or a high-ranking official.
    • 贺若弼 - A historical figure, a general during the Sui Dynasty.
    • 长孙鸾 - A historical figure, a high-ranking official during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 侍郎 - A title for a high-ranking official.

    The Recorder, who was light-hearted and quick-witted, outdid everyone else and decided to fine Lady Cai, saying, "Uncle Liu is talented and handsome, why didn't you give him the role of Five-Path Master, leaving him to pretend as a Judge, fearing Aunt Six would be unhappy, so he must drink a cup deeply." Lady Cai immediately took the cup and said, "I truly know I am being punished, but it's because Uncle Liu is old and has weak eyesight, and he might not be able to read the important yellow paper documents, causing a problem for the high-ranking official. Drinking a cup is no harm." The other ladies all laughed heartily. Then another lady stood up, passing on a verbal command, still pulling out a green hairpin, and said quickly, "The green hairpin must be passed, if it's not passed, the command is invalid and you will be punished." The command was, "Old Master Lu has a good mind, a good mind Old Master Lu." The command was passed around several times, and then the order was given for Lady Zi Sui to sit down and recite the command. Lady Zi Sui, who was usually hesitant and slow to speak, when it was her turn, could only repeat, "Old Master Lu, Old Master Lu." All the ladies laughed and said, "In the past, General He Ruobing played with Cao Luan, the high-ranking official, because he was old, stuttered, and was bald, so he invented this command." [58]

    三更后，皆弹琴击筑，齐唱迭和。歌曰：
    　　明日清风，良宵会同。星河易翻，欢娱不终。
    　　绿樽翠杓，为君斟酌。今夕不饮，何时欢乐？又歌曰：
    　　杨柳杨柳，袅袅随风急。
    　　西楼美人春梦中，翠帘斜卷千条人。
    　　又歌曰：
    　　玉户金釭，愿陪君王。邯郸宫中，金石丝簧。
    　　卫女秦娥，左右成行。纨缟缤纷，翠眉红妆。
    　　王欢转盼，为王歌舞。愿得君欢，常无灾苦。 [59]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [59]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 三更后 - Refers to the third watch of the night, typically from 11 PM to 1 AM in the traditional Chinese time system.
    • 皆弹琴击筑 - Describes a scene where everyone is playing the qin (a type of zither) and the zhu (a type of lute).
    • 齐唱迭和 - Means 'singing in unison and taking turns,' indicating a harmonious and alternating singing pattern.
    • 明日清风，良宵会同 - A poetic expression suggesting a pleasant evening gathering with clear skies and gentle breezes.
    • 星河易翻，欢娱不终 - '星河' refers to the Milky Way, and '易翻' suggests the passage of time. The phrase implies that joyful moments are fleeting.
    • 绿樽翠杓，为君斟酌 - '绿樽' and '翠杓' are poetic terms for green wine cups and jade ladles, respectively. '为君斟酌' means 'to pour wine for you.'
    • 今夕不饮，何时欢乐 - A rhetorical question emphasizing the importance of enjoying the present moment.
    • 杨柳杨柳，袅袅随风急 - Describes willow trees swaying gently in the wind, a common motif in classical Chinese poetry.
    • 西楼美人春梦中，翠帘斜卷千条人 - A poetic description of a beautiful woman in a dream, with her green curtains fluttering and her hair (or perhaps a long garment) flowing like a thousand strands.
    • 玉户金釭，愿陪君王 - '玉户' and '金釭' refer to a jade door and a golden lamp, respectively. The phrase expresses a wish to accompany the king.
    • 邯郸宫中，金石丝簧 - Describes the luxurious music and instruments in the Han Palace at Handan.
    • 卫女秦娥，左右成行 - Refers to the beautiful women of Wei and Qin, arranged in rows on either side.
    • 纨缟缤纷，翠眉红妆 - Describes the colorful silk garments and the green brows and red makeup of the women.
    • 王欢转盼，为王歌舞 - Describes the king's joy and the women's dance and song.
    • 愿得君欢，常无灾苦 - A wish for the king's happiness and the absence of misfortune.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 三更后 - The third watch of the night, a significant time in traditional Chinese culture.
    • 弹琴击筑 - The qin and zhu are traditional Chinese musical instruments, often associated with scholars and poets.
    • 齐唱迭和 - A common form of group singing in classical Chinese literature, emphasizing harmony and unity.
    • 星河 - The Milky Way, often used in poetry to symbolize the vastness of the universe and the passage of time.
    • 绿樽翠杓 - Poetic terms for wine cups and ladles, often used in classical Chinese poetry to describe luxurious settings.
    • 杨柳 - Willow trees, a common symbol of parting and longing in Chinese poetry.
    • 西楼美人 - A reference to a beautiful woman in a dream, often used in classical Chinese poetry to evoke romantic or nostalgic themes.
    • 邯郸宫 - The Han Palace at Handan, a famous palace in Chinese history.
    • 卫女秦娥 - Beautiful women from the states of Wei and Qin, often used in classical Chinese literature to describe the beauty and grace of women.
    • 纨缟 - Colorful silk garments, often used to describe the luxurious clothing of noblewomen.
    • 翠眉红妆 - Green brows and red makeup, typical of the beauty standards of the time.
    • 王欢转盼 - The king's joy and the women's dance and song, often used to describe the entertainment and pleasure of the royal court.
    • 愿得君欢，常无灾苦 - A wish for the king's happiness and the absence of misfortune.

    Proper Nouns

    • 三更 - The third watch of the night.
    • 星河 - The Milky Way.
    • 绿樽 - Green wine cup.
    • 翠杓 - Jade ladle.
    • 杨柳 - Willow tree.
    • 西楼 - A specific location, possibly a pavilion or tower.
    • 邯郸宫 - The Han Palace at Handan.
    • 卫女 - Beautiful women from the state of Wei.
    • 秦娥 - Beautiful women from the state of Qin.
    • 纨缟 - Colorful silk garments.
    • 翠眉 - Green brows.
    • 红妆 - Red makeup.
    • 王 - The king.

    After the third watch, everyone played the qin and zhu, singing in unison and taking turns. They sang:

    "Tomorrow, the wind will be clear, and this fine evening will bring us together. The Milky Way shifts easily, and joy is fleeting.
    With green cups and jade ladles, I pour wine for you. If we don't drink tonight, when will we find joy?" Another song was sung:

    "Willow, willow, swaying gently in the wind.
    In a dream in the west pavilion, a beautiful woman lies with her green curtains fluttering, her hair or garment flowing like a thousand strands."
    Another song was sung:

    "Jade doors and golden lamps, I wish to accompany the king. In the Han Palace at Handan, the music of gold, stone, silk, and bamboo resounds.
    The beautiful women of Wei and Qin stand in rows on either side. Their colorful silk garments and green brows and red makeup are a sight to behold.
    The king's joy is evident in his gaze, and the women dance and sing for him. May the king always be happy and free from misfortune." [59]

    歌竟，已是四更。即有一黄衫人，头有角，仪貌甚伟，走入拜曰：“婆提王 屈娘子，便请娘子速来！”女郎等皆起而受命，却传曰：“不知王见召，适相与 望月至此。既蒙王呼唤，敢不奔赴。”因命青衣收拾盘筵。讽因大声连咳，视庭 中无复一物。明旦，谛视之，拾得翠钗数只。将出示人，更不知是何物也。
    　　○董慎
    　　隋大业元年，兖州佐史董慎，性公直，明法理。自都督以下，用法有不直， 必起犯颜而谏之。虽加削责，亦不惧，必俟刑正而后退。尝因事暇偶归家，出州 门，逢一黄衣使者曰：“太山府君呼君为录事，知之乎？”因出怀中牒示慎。牒 曰：“董慎名称茂实，案牒精练，将分疑狱，必俟良能，权差知右曹录事者。” 印处分明，及后署曰倨。慎谓使者曰：“府君呼我，岂有不行，然不识府君名谓 何？”使者曰：“录事勿言，到府即知矣。”因持大布囊，内慎于中，负之趋出 兖州郭，致囊于路左，汲水为泥，封慎两目。 [60]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [60]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 歌竟，已是四更 - Indicates that the song has ended and it is now the fourth watch of the night.
    • 黄衫人，头有角，仪貌甚伟 - Describes a man wearing a yellow robe with horns, who is very imposing in appearance.
    • 婆提王 屈娘子，便请娘子速来 - A formal invitation from King Bodi, asking the lady to come quickly.
    • 不知王见召，适相与望月至此 - An explanation that they were unaware of the king's summons and had just come to admire the moon.
    • 既蒙王呼唤，敢不奔赴 - A polite expression of willingness to respond to the king's call.
    • 讽因大声连咳，视庭中无复一物 - A description of someone coughing loudly, only to find nothing left in the courtyard.
    • 明旦，谛视之，拾得翠钗数只 - The next morning, upon careful examination, several jade hairpins are found.
    • 将出示人，更不知是何物也 - The intention to show these items to others, but not knowing what they are.
    • 隋大业元年 - The year 605 AD during the Sui Dynasty.
    • 兖州佐史董慎 - A government official named Dong Shen serving in Yanzhou.
    • 性公直，明法理 - Describes Dong Shen as being upright and knowledgeable in legal matters.
    • 自都督以下，用法有不直，必起犯颜而谏之 - If any subordinate official uses the law unfairly, Dong Shen would boldly advise them.
    • 虽加削责，亦不惧，必俟刑正而后退 - Even if punished, he would not be afraid and would wait for justice to be served.
    • 尝因事暇偶归家，出州门，逢一黄衣使者曰 - On a day when he was free and returning home, he met a messenger dressed in yellow.
    • 太山府君呼君为录事，知之乎 - The messenger says that the Lord of Tai Mountain has called him to serve as a recorder.
    • 因出怀中牒示慎 - The messenger shows a document from his chest.
    • 董慎名称茂实，案牒精练，将分疑狱，必俟良能，权差知右曹录事者 - The document praises Dong Shen's reputation and skills and appoints him as a recorder.
    • 印处分明，及后署曰倨 - The seal is clear, and the document is signed 'A'.
    • 府君呼我，岂有不行，然不识府君名谓何 - Dong Shen asks if he should go to the lord, but does not know the lord's name.
    • 录事勿言，到府即知矣 - The messenger tells Dong Shen not to worry and that he will know at the lord's place.
    • 因持大布囊，内慎于中，负之趋出兖州郭，致囊于路左，汲水为泥，封慎两目 - The messenger places Dong Shen in a large sack, carries him out of the city, and seals his eyes with mud.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 歌竟，已是四更 - Refers to the traditional Chinese timekeeping system where the night is divided into five watches.
    • 黄衫人，头有角，仪貌甚伟 - Describes a supernatural or divine figure, possibly a deity or spirit.
    • 婆提王 - Refers to King Bodi, a figure from Buddhist mythology.
    • 太山府君 - Refers to the Lord of Tai Mountain, a deity in Chinese folklore.
    • 隋大业元年 - The year 605 AD during the Sui Dynasty, a significant period in Chinese history.
    • 兖州佐史董慎 - A government official in the Sui Dynasty, indicating the political context of the story.
    • 性公直，明法理 - Reflects the ideal of an upright and knowledgeable official in Chinese bureaucracy.
    • 自都督以下，用法有不直，必起犯颜而谏之 - Shows the importance of justice and the role of officials in maintaining it.
    • 虽加削责，亦不惧，必俟刑正而后退 - Highlights the courage and integrity of officials in the face of punishment.
    • 尝因事暇偶归家，出州门，逢一黄衣使者曰 - Describes a common scenario in Chinese literature where officials encounter supernatural beings.
    • 太山府君呼君为录事，知之乎 - The Lord of Tai Mountain is a significant figure in Chinese mythology, often associated with judgment and authority.
    • 因出怀中牒示慎 - The use of a document to convey a message is a common literary device in Chinese texts.
    • 董慎名称茂实，案牒精练，将分疑狱，必俟良能，权差知右曹录事者 - The document praises Dong Shen's reputation and skills, reflecting the importance of merit in Chinese society.
    • 印处分明，及后署曰倨 - The seal and signature add authenticity to the document, a common practice in official documents.
    • 府君呼我，岂有不行，然不识府君名谓何 - The uncertainty about the lord's name adds a layer of mystery to the story.
    • 录事勿言，到府即知矣 - The messenger's reassurance reflects the trust and authority of the supernatural figure.
    • 因持大布囊，内慎于中，负之趋出兖州郭，致囊于路左，汲水为泥，封慎两目 - The supernatural transportation and sealing of Dong Shen's eyes add a fantastical element to the story.

    Proper Nouns

    • 婆提王 - King Bodi, a figure from Buddhist mythology.
    • 太山府君 - The Lord of Tai Mountain, a deity in Chinese folklore.
    • 董慎 - The main character, a government official in the Sui Dynasty.
    • 隋大业元年 - The year 605 AD during the Sui Dynasty.
    • 兖州 - A region in China during the Sui Dynasty.
    • 录事 - A recorder or secretary in a government office.

    The song ended, and it was already the fourth watch of the night. Suddenly, a man in a yellow robe, with horns on his head and an imposing appearance, entered and bowed, saying, "King Bodi summons Lady, please come quickly!" The young ladies all rose and accepted the command, then replied, "We did not know the king had summoned us; we just came here to admire the moon. Having been called by the king, how could we not hasten to his presence?" They then ordered the servants to clear the table. One of them coughed loudly, and when they looked around the courtyard, there was nothing left. The next morning, upon careful examination, they found several jade hairpins. They intended to show these to others, but did not know what they were.

    ○ Dong Shen

    In the first year of the Daoyi era of the Sui Dynasty, Dong Shen, an assistant official in Yanzhou, was upright and knowledgeable in legal matters. If any subordinate official used the law unfairly, he would boldly advise them. Even if punished, he would not be afraid and would wait for justice to be served before retreating. One day, when he was free and returning home, he met a messenger dressed in yellow who said, "The Lord of Tai Mountain has called you to serve as a recorder, do you know this?" The messenger then showed a document from his chest. The document read, "Dong Shen is renowned for his reputation and solid achievements, and his case files are well-prepared. He is to be appointed as a recorder in the right department." The seal was clear, and the document was signed "A." Dong Shen asked the messenger, "The lord calls me, how could I not go? But I do not know the lord's name." The messenger replied, "Recorder, do not worry; you will know at the lord's place." The messenger then took a large sack, placed Dong Shen inside, carried him out of the city, and sealed his eyes with mud. [60]

    慎目既无所睹，都不知经过远近，忽闻大唱曰：“范慎追董慎到。”使者曰： “诺。”趋入。府君曰：“所追录事，今复何在？”使者曰：“冥司幽秘，恐或 漏泄，向请左曹匿影布囊盛之。”府君大笑曰：“使一范慎追一董慎，取左曹布 囊盛一右曹录事，可谓能防慎矣。”便令写出，抉去目泥，便赐青缣衣、鱼须笏、 豹皮靴，文甚斑驳。邀登副阶，命左右取榻令坐，曰：“藉君公正，故有是请。 今有闽州司马令狐寔等六人，置无间狱，承天曹符，以寔是太元夫人三等亲，准 令式递减三等。昨罪人程翥一百二十人引例，喧讼纷纭，不可止遏。已具名申天 曹。天曹以为罚疑唯轻，亦令量减二等。余恐后人引例多矣，君谓宜如何？”慎 曰：“夫水照妍蚩而人不怒者，以其至清无情，况于天地刑法，岂宜恩贷奸慝。 然慎一胥吏尔，素无文字，虽知不可，终语无条贯。常州府秀才张审通，辞彩隽 拔，足得备君管记。”府君令帖召。 [61]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [61]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 慎目既无所睹 - Literal meaning: 'Shen's eyes saw nothing.' The exact meaning of '慎目' is unclear, but it likely refers to a person named Fan Shen or Dong Shen.
    • 都不知经过远近 - Literal meaning: 'Completely unaware of the distance traveled.'
    • 忽闻大唱曰 - Literal meaning: 'Suddenly heard a loud call saying.'
    • 范慎追董慎到 - Literal meaning: 'Fan Shen pursued Dong Shen to a place.' The exact relationship between Fan Shen and Dong Shen is unclear.
    • 使者曰：“诺。”趋入 - Literal meaning: 'The messenger said, “Yes,” and hurriedly entered.'
    • 府君曰 - Literal meaning: 'The official said.' '府君' refers to a high-ranking official or magistrate.
    • 所追录事，今复何在 - Literal meaning: 'The person being pursued (as a recorder), where is he now?'
    • 冥司幽秘，恐或漏泄 - Literal meaning: 'The underworld is secret, fearing leakage or exposure.' '冥司' refers to the underworld or hell.
    • 向请左曹匿影布囊盛之 - Literal meaning: 'Previously requested the Left Department to hide the shadow in a cloth bag and contain it.' '左曹' refers to a specific department or office.
    • 使一范慎追一董慎，取左曹布囊盛一右曹录事 - Literal meaning: 'Sent one Fan Shen to pursue one Dong Shen, taking the Left Department's cloth bag to contain one Right Department's recorder.' The exact meaning of '匿影布囊' is unclear.
    • 可谓能防慎矣 - Literal meaning: 'Can be said to be able to prevent caution.' The exact meaning of '防慎' is unclear.
    • 便令写出，抉去目泥，便赐青缣衣、鱼须笏、豹皮靴 - Literal meaning: 'Immediately ordered to write out, remove the mud from the eyes, and grant a blue silk robe, fish whisker tablet, and leopard skin boots.' The exact meaning of '抉去目泥' is unclear.
    • 邀登副阶，命左右取榻令坐 - Literal meaning: 'Invited to ascend the secondary platform, ordered the attendants to fetch a couch and sit.' The exact meaning of '副阶' is unclear.
    • 藉君公正，故有是请 - Literal meaning: 'Because of your fairness, there is this request.' The exact meaning of '是请' is unclear.
    • 今有闽州司马令狐寔等六人，置无间狱 - Literal meaning: 'Now there are six people including Sima Lingfu from Minzhou, placed in a no-judgment prison.' '闽州' is a place name, '司马' is an official title, and '令狐寔' is a proper name.
    • 承天曹符，以寔是太元夫人三等亲，准令式递减三等 - Literal meaning: 'Received a decree from the celestial department, because X is a third-degree relative of Lady Taiyuan, according to the regulations, the punishment is reduced by three degrees.' '天曹' refers to the celestial department, '太元夫人' is a title or name, possibly referring to a noblewoman or deity.
    • 昨罪人程翥一百二十人引例，喧讼纷纭，不可止遏 - Literal meaning: 'Yesterday's criminals, Cheng Zhu and one hundred and twenty others, cited examples, noisy and chaotic litigation, cannot be stopped.' '程翥' is a proper name, possibly referring to a criminal or a historical figure.
    • 已具名申天曹 - Literal meaning: 'Already listed the names and submitted to the celestial department.' '天曹' refers to the celestial department.
    • 天曹以为罚疑唯轻，亦令量减二等 - Literal meaning: 'The celestial department considered the punishment should be lenient in doubt, also ordered a reduction of two degrees.'
    • 余恐后人引例多矣，君谓宜如何 - Literal meaning: 'I fear that later people will cite many examples, what do you think should be done?'
    • 夫水照妍蚩而人不怒者，以其至清无情，况于天地刑法，岂宜恩贷奸慝 - Literal meaning: 'Water reflects beauty and ugliness, and people do not get angry, because it is extremely clear and impartial, how much more so for heavenly laws, how can they be lenient with wickedness?'
    • 然慎一胥吏尔，素无文字，虽知不可，终语无条贯 - Literal meaning: 'But Shen is just a minor official, has never written anything, even though he knows it is wrong, his words lack coherence.' '慎' refers to a minor official, '胥吏' means a low-ranking official.
    • 常州府秀才张审通，辞彩隽拔，足得备君管记 - Literal meaning: 'Scholar Zhang Shentong from Changzhou Prefecture, eloquent and outstanding, is qualified to serve as your secretary.' '常州府' is a place name, '秀才' means a scholar, '张审通' is a proper name.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 范慎追董慎到 - The exact relationship between Fan Shen and Dong Shen is unclear, but it suggests a pursuit or chase.
    • 府君 - Refers to a high-ranking official or magistrate.
    • 左曹 - Refers to a specific department or office in the bureaucracy.
    • 右曹 - Refers to another specific department or office in the bureaucracy.
    • 闽州司马令狐寔 - '闽州' is a place name, '司马' is an official title, and '令狐寔' is a proper name.
    • 太元夫人 - '太元夫人' is a title or name, possibly referring to a noblewoman or deity.
    • 天曹 - Refers to the celestial department or a higher authority in the bureaucracy.
    • 程翥 - A proper name, possibly referring to a criminal or a historical figure.
    • 常州府 - A place name, referring to a city or administrative region.
    • 张审通 - A proper name, referring to a scholar or official.

    Once Fan Shen's eyes were opened, he saw nothing, completely unaware of how far he had traveled. Suddenly, he heard a loud call: "Fan Shen, pursue Dong Shen to his destination." The messenger replied, "Yes," and hurriedly entered. The magistrate asked, "Where is the recorder we were pursuing?" The messenger replied, "The underworld is secretive, and there is fear of leakage or exposure. Previously, I requested the Left Department to hide the shadow in a cloth bag and contain him." The magistrate laughed and said, "To send one Fan Shen to pursue one Dong Shen, and to take a cloth bag from the Left Department to contain a recorder from the Right Department—this can be said to be able to prevent caution." He immediately ordered the recorder to be written out, the mud from his eyes removed, and then granted him a blue silk robe, a fish whisker tablet, and leopard skin boots, which were quite mottled. He invited Fan Shen to ascend to the secondary platform, ordered the attendants to fetch a couch for him to sit on, and said, "Because of your fairness, I have this request. Now, there are six people, including the governor of Minzhou, Lingfu Shi, who are placed in a no-judgment prison. According to a decree from the celestial department, since Shi is a third-degree relative of Lady Taiyuan, the punishment is reduced by three degrees according to the regulations. Yesterday, one hundred and twenty criminals, including Cheng Zhu, cited examples, causing noisy and chaotic litigation that could not be stopped. The names have already been listed and submitted to the celestial department. The celestial department considered that punishment should be lenient in doubt and also ordered a reduction of two degrees. I fear that later people will cite many examples. What do you think should be done?" Fan Shen said, "Water reflects beauty and ugliness, and people do not get angry because it is extremely clear and impartial. How much more so for heavenly laws? How can they be lenient with wickedness? But I am just a minor official, have never written anything, and even though I know it is wrong, my words lack coherence. The scholar Zhang Shentong from Changzhou is eloquent and outstanding and is qualified to serve as your secretary." The magistrate ordered a summons to be issued. [61]

    俄顷审通至，曰：“此易耳，君当判以状申。”府君曰：“尹善为我辞。” 即补充左曹录事，仍赐衣服如董慎，各给一玄狐，每出即乘之。审通判曰：“天 本无私，法宜画一，苟从恩贷，是恣奸行。令狐寔前命减刑，已同私请；程翥后 申簿诉，且异罪疑。倘开递减之科，实失公家之论。请依前付无间狱，仍录状申 天曹者。”即有黄衫人持状而往。少顷，复持天符曰：“所申文状，多起异端。 奉主之宜，但合遵守。周礼八议，一曰议亲，又元化匮中释冲符，亦曰无不亲。 是则典章昭然，有何不可。岂可使太元功德，不能庇三等之亲。仍敢愆违，须有 惩谪。府君可罚不紫衣六十甲子，余依前处分者。”府君大怒审通曰：“君为情 辞，使我受谴。”即命左右取方寸肉塞却一耳，遂无闻。审通诉曰：“乞更为判 申，不允，则甘罪再罚。 [62]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [62]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 俄顷 - Temporal phrase meaning 'in a short while' or 'soon.'
    • 此易耳 - Colloquial expression meaning 'this is easy.'
    • 君当判以状申 - Means 'you should judge and submit a report.'
    • 尹善为我辞 - Means 'Yi should speak well for me.'
    • 即补充左曹录事 - Means 'immediately appointed as Left Censor.'
    • 仍赐衣服如董慎 - Means 'and was also given clothes like those of Dong Shen.'
    • 各给一玄狐 - Means 'each was given one black fox.'
    • 每出即乘之 - Means 'each time they went out, they rode it.'
    • 天本无私 - Means 'heaven is originally impartial.'
    • 法宜画一 - Means 'the law should be uniform.'
    • 苟从恩贷 - Means 'if one grants mercy.'
    • 是恣奸行 - Means 'it would be to indulge in wicked deeds.'
    • 令狐寔前命减刑 - Means 'Linghu Shi was previously ordered to reduce the sentence.'
    • 程翥后申簿诉 - Means 'Cheng Zhu later submitted a petition.'
    • 且异罪疑 - Means 'and there is a difference in doubt.'
    • 倘开递减之科 - Means 'if one opens the door to successive reductions.'
    • 实失公家之论 - Means 'it would be to lose the public's opinion.'
    • 请依前付无间狱 - Means 'please follow the previous order to send to the hell of no return.'
    • 仍录状申天曹者 - Means 'and record the report to be submitted to the heavenly court.'
    • 即有黄衫人持状而往 - Means 'there was a man in yellow clothes who took the report and went.'
    • 少顷，复持天符曰 - Means 'soon, he returned with a heavenly decree saying.'
    • 所申文状，多起异端 - Means 'the submitted report contains many heretical things.'
    • 奉主之宜，但合遵守 - Means 'in accordance with the master's will, it should only be followed.'
    • 周礼八议 - Refers to the 'Eight Deliberations' in the *Rites of Zhou*, a system for mitigating punishment for certain individuals.
    • 一曰议亲 - Means 'the first is deliberation for relatives.'
    • 元化匮中释冲符 - Refers to a text or commentary, possibly a Daoist text, where '元化匮' and '释冲符' are titles or names.
    • 无不亲 - Means 'there is no one who is not a relative.'
    • 典章昭然 - Means 'the rules are clearly established.'
    • 有何不可 - Means 'what is there to not accept?'
    • 岂可使太元功德，不能庇三等之亲 - Means 'how can the great virtue of Taiyuan not protect three grades of relatives?'
    • 仍敢愆违 - Means 'still dare to violate.'
    • 须有惩谪 - Means 'there must be punishment.'
    • 府君可罚不紫衣六十甲子 - Means 'the magistrate should be punished by not wearing purple robes for sixty years.'
    • 余依前处分者 - Means 'the rest should follow the previous disposition.'
    • 君为情辞 - Means 'you speak out of sentiment.'
    • 使我受谴 - Means 'making me suffer punishment.'
    • 即命左右取方寸肉塞却一耳 - Means 'he immediately ordered his attendants to take a piece of flesh and plug one of his ears.'
    • 遂无闻 - Means 'so he could no longer hear.'
    • 乞更为判申 - Means 'I beg you to judge and submit again.'
    • 不允，则甘罪再罚 - Means 'if you do not agree, I will willingly accept the punishment again.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 审通 - A proper name, likely a historical figure or fictional character in the context of the story.
    • 尹善 - A proper name, likely a historical figure or fictional character in the context of the story.
    • 董慎 - A proper name, likely a historical figure or fictional character in the context of the story.
    • 令狐寔 - A proper name, likely a historical figure or fictional character in the context of the story.
    • 程翥 - A proper name, likely a historical figure or fictional character in the context of the story.
    • 周礼八议 - A system of mitigating punishment for certain individuals, as described in the *Rites of Zhou*.
    • 元化匮中释冲符 - A reference to a text or commentary, possibly a Daoist text, where '元化匮' and '释冲符' are titles or names.
    • 太元功德 - Refers to the great virtue of Taiyuan, a place or concept in Chinese mythology or history.
    • 三等之亲 - Refers to three grades of relatives, indicating a hierarchical system of kinship.
    • 紫衣 - Refers to purple robes, often associated with high-ranking officials or nobility in Chinese culture.
    • 六十甲子 - Refers to the sixty-year cycle in the Chinese zodiac, often used in timekeeping and astrology.
    • 无间狱 - Refers to the hell of no return, a concept in Chinese mythology and Buddhism.
    • 天曹 - Refers to the heavenly court, a concept in Chinese mythology and religion.
    • 黄衫人 - Refers to a man in yellow clothes, possibly a messenger or official.
    • 天符 - Refers to a heavenly decree, a concept in Chinese mythology and religion.
    • 府君 - Refers to a magistrate or local official, a common title in Chinese history.

    Proper Nouns

    • 审通 - A proper name, likely a historical figure or fictional character in the context of the story.
    • 尹善 - A proper name, likely a historical figure or fictional character in the context of the story.
    • 董慎 - A proper name, likely a historical figure or fictional character in the context of the story.
    • 令狐寔 - A proper name, likely a historical figure or fictional character in the context of the story.
    • 程翥 - A proper name, likely a historical figure or fictional character in the context of the story.
    • 府君 - A title, likely referring to a magistrate or local official.
    • 周礼八议 - A system of mitigating punishment for certain individuals, as described in the *Rites of Zhou*.
    • 元化匮中释冲符 - A reference to a text or commentary, possibly a Daoist text, where '元化匮' and '释冲符' are titles or names.

    Soon, Shentong arrived and said, "This is easy. You should judge and submit a report." The magistrate said, "Yi should speak well for me." Immediately, he was appointed as Left Censor and was also given clothes like those of Dong Shen, each was given one black fox, and each time they went out, they rode it. Shentong judged and said, "Heaven is originally impartial. The law should be uniform. If one grants mercy, it would be to indulge in wicked deeds. Linghu Shi was previously ordered to reduce the sentence, which is the same as a private request; Cheng Zhu later submitted a petition, and there is a difference in doubt. If one opens the door to successive reductions, it would be to lose the public's opinion. Please follow the previous order to send to the hell of no return, and record the report to be submitted to the heavenly court." There was a man in yellow clothes who took the report and went. Soon, he returned with a heavenly decree saying, "The submitted report contains many heretical things. In accordance with the master's will, it should only be followed. The Rites of Zhou have the Eight Deliberations, the first is deliberation for relatives. The Yuanhua Kui in Shi Chongfu also says there is no one who is not a relative. The rules are clearly established, what is there to not accept? How can the great virtue of Taiyuan not protect three grades of relatives? Still dare to violate, there must be punishment. The magistrate should be punished by not wearing purple robes for sixty years. The rest should follow the previous disposition." The magistrate was very angry at Shentong and said, "You speak out of sentiment, making me suffer punishment." He immediately ordered his attendants to take a piece of flesh and plug one of his ears, so he could no longer hear. Shentong pleaded, "I beg you to judge and submit again. If you do not agree, I will willingly accept the punishment again." [62]

    ”府君曰：“君为我去罪，即更与君一耳。”审通又判 曰：“天大地大，本以无亲；若使奉主，何由得一？苟欲因情变法，实将生伪丧 真。太古以前，人犹至朴，中古之降，方闻各亲。岂可使太古育物之心，生仲尼 观蜡之叹。无不亲，是非公也，何必引之。请宽逆耳之辜，敢荐沃心之药。庶其 阅实，用得平均。令狐寔等并请依正法。仍录状申天曹者。”黄衣人又持往，须 臾又有天符来曰：“再省所申，甚为允当。府君可加六天副正使，令狐寔、程翥 等并正法处置者。”府君悦，即谓审通曰：“非君不可以正此狱。”因命左右割 下耳中肉，令一小儿擘之为一耳，安于审通额上，曰：“塞君一耳，与君三耳， 何如？”又谓慎曰：“甚赖君荐贤以成我美，然不可久留君，当寿一周年相报耳。 君兼本寿，得二十一年矣。”即促送归家。 [63]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [63]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 于是 - Transitional phrase meaning 'then' or 'at this point.'
    • 天帝命黄衣使者持符来曰 - Formal expression meaning 'the Heavenly Emperor commanded a yellow-robed messenger to come with a talisman and say.'
    • 府君所判，皆合天心 - Phrase meaning 'the magistrate's judgments all align with the will of heaven.'
    • 今令狐寔等并依正法处置 - Phrase meaning 'now, Linghu Shi and others will be dealt with according to the proper law.'
    • 仍录状申天曹者 - Phrase meaning 'and record the case for submission to the heavenly court.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 天帝 - Refers to the Heavenly Emperor, a deity in Chinese mythology.
    • 黄衣使者 - Refers to a yellow-robed messenger, often associated with divine or imperial authority.
    • 府君 - Refers to a local magistrate or official.
    • 令狐寔 - A historical figure, likely a person of importance in the story.
    • 天曹 - Refers to the heavenly court or the realm of the gods.

    The magistrate said, "If you remove my sins, then give me one more chance." Shentong then ruled, "Heaven and earth are vast, but they are fundamentally impartial; if it is to serve the ruler, how could one achieve unity? If one wishes to change the law based on emotions, it will inevitably lead to falsehood and the loss of truth. Before the ancient times, people were still very simple; in the middle ages, one began to hear about various forms of kinship. How can one allow the ancient heart of nurturing all things to give rise to Confucius's sigh at the sacrificial ceremony? If there is no distinction in kinship, it is not fair or just; why should one follow it? Please forgive the offense of hearing unpleasant truths, and I dare to recommend a remedy for the heart. Perhaps by examining the facts, fairness can be achieved. I ask that Linghu Shi and others be dealt with according to the proper law. And record the case for submission to the heavenly court." A yellow-robed messenger took it away, and shortly thereafter another heavenly edict arrived, saying, "Upon reviewing what has been submitted, it is indeed appropriate. The magistrate can be appointed as a deputy to the Six Heavenly Officials. Linghu Shi, Cheng Zhu, and others will be dealt with according to the proper law." The magistrate was pleased and said to Shentong, "Without you, this case could not have been resolved." He then ordered his attendants to cut a piece of flesh from his own ear and had a small child divide it into one ear, which he placed on Shentong's forehead, saying, "I block one of your ears and give you three ears; how is that?" He also said to Shentong, "I greatly rely on your recommendation of worthy individuals to complete my good deeds, but I cannot keep you here for long; I will reward you with an additional year of life. You will live for twenty-one years in total." He then immediately sent him back home. [63]

    使者复以泥封二人，布囊各送至宅，欻如写出，而顾问妻子，妻子云：“君 亡精魂已十余日矣。”慎自此果二十一年而卒。审通数日额角痒，遂踊出一耳， 通前三耳，而踊出者尤聪。时人笑曰：“天有九头鸟，地有三耳秀才。”亦呼为 鸡冠秀才者。慎初见府君称邻，后方知倨乃邻家也。
    　　
    　　卷三
    　　
    　　○开元明皇幸广陵
    　　
    　　开元十八年正月望夕，帝谓叶仙师曰：“四方之盛，陈于此夕，师知何处极 丽？”对曰：“灯烛华丽，百戏陈设，士女争妍，粉黛相染，天下无逾于广陵矣。” 帝曰：“何术可使吾一观之？”师曰：“待御皆可，何独陛下乎。”俄而虹桥起 于殿前，板阁架虚，栏楯若画。师奏：“桥成，请行，但无回顾而已。”于是帝 步而上之，太真及侍臣高力士、黄幡绰、乐官数十人从行，步步渐高，若造云中。 [64]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [64]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 泥封 - Technical term for sealing with clay, often used in official documents.
    • 布囊 - Formal and respectful method of delivery using a cloth bag.
    • 欻如写出 - Sudden and unexpected event.
    • 顾问 - Formal or respectful inquiry.
    • 君亡精魂已十余日矣 - Metaphorical way of saying the person has been dead for more than ten days.
    • 慎自此果二十一年而卒 - Long period of time.
    • 审通数日额角痒 - Literal description of a physical sensation.
    • 遂踊出一耳 - Metaphorical or supernatural event.
    • 通前三耳 - Total count of ears.
    • 天有九头鸟，地有三耳秀才 - Metaphorical comparison to emphasize the unusual nature of Shen Tong.
    • 鸡冠秀才 - Metaphorical description, possibly referring to a prominent or unusual feature.
    • 慎初见府君称邻 - Formal or respectful way of addressing someone.
    • 后方知倨乃邻家也 - Indicates a realization or discovery.
    • 开元十八年正月望夕 - Specific date in the Chinese calendar.
    • 帝谓叶仙师曰 - The emperor addressing a Taoist master.
    • 四方之盛，陈于此夕 - Poetic way of describing a festive occasion.
    • 师知何处极丽 - Formal and respectful way of asking.
    • 灯烛华丽，百戏陈设 - Describes a festive scene.
    • 士女争妍，粉黛相染 - Vivid description of a festive gathering.
    • 天下无逾于广陵矣 - Hyperbolic statement emphasizing Guangling's splendor.
    • 何术可使吾一观之 - Formal and respectful way of asking.
    • 待御皆可，何独陛下乎 - Rhetorical question emphasizing equality.
    • 俄而虹桥起于殿前 - Poetic and fantastical description.
    • 板阁架虚，栏楯若画 - Detailed and poetic description of a fantastical structure.
    • 师奏：“桥成，请行，但无回顾而已” - Formal and instructive statement.
    • 于是帝步而上之 - Physical action reflecting imperial presence and authority.
    • 太真及侍臣高力士、黄幡绰、乐官数十人从行 - List of people accompanying the emperor.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 开元十八年正月望夕 - Specific date in the reign of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
    • 帝谓叶仙师曰 - Reflects the importance of Taoism in Tang dynasty culture.
    • 四方之盛，陈于此夕 - Common theme in classical Chinese literature and art.
    • 灯烛华丽，百戏陈设 - Typical of imperial celebrations.
    • 士女争妍，粉黛相染 - Reflects the social customs of the time.
    • 天下无逾于广陵矣 - Guangling, a city known for its cultural and economic significance during the Tang dynasty.
    • 何术可使吾一观之 - The emperor's desire to see a spectacle, reflecting the imperial interest in grand events.
    • 待御皆可，何独陛下乎 - Emphasizes equality, reflecting the Taoist philosophy of harmony and balance.
    • 俄而虹桥起于殿前 - Common motif in Chinese mythology and literature, symbolizing a connection between the earthly and the divine.
    • 板阁架虚，栏楯若画 - Reflects the rich imagination of classical Chinese literature.
    • 师奏：“桥成，请行，但无回顾而已” - Reflects the formal and ritualistic nature of Taoist practices.
    • 于是帝步而上之 - Reflects the importance of imperial presence and authority.
    • 太真及侍臣高力士、黄幡绰、乐官数十人从行 - Reflects the imperial court's structure and personnel.

    The envoy sealed two people with clay and sent each in a cloth bag to their homes, as if suddenly written out. He then asked his wife, and she replied, "Your spirit has left for over ten days." Shen indeed died twenty-one years later. Shen Tong felt itching on his forehead for several days, and suddenly a new ear grew out, making him have three ears in total, with the new one being especially intelligent. People at the time laughed and said, "Heaven has a nine-headed bird, and earth has a three-eared scholar." They also called him the Scholar with a Chicken's Comb. Shen initially called the magistrate "neighbor," but later realized that Jù was his neighbor.

    Volume Three
    Chapter on Emperor Xuanzong's Visit to Guangling
    In the eighteenth year of the Kaiyuan era, on the night of the full moon in the first month, the emperor said to Leaf Master, "The grandeur of the four directions is displayed on this night. Teacher, do you know where it is most splendid?" The master replied, "The lights and candles are splendid, and various performances are set up. Men and women vie for beauty, their faces adorned with rouge and powder. There is none more splendid than Guangling in the world." The emperor asked, "What method can allow me to see it once?" The master said, "Anyone can wait for it, why only Your Majesty?" Suddenly, a rainbow bridge rose in front of the palace, its wooden pavilion built on empty space, its railings like paintings. The master said, "The bridge is ready, please go, but do not look back." Thus, the emperor stepped onto it, followed by Tai Zhen and the attendants, including Gao Lishi, Huang Fanchuo, and dozens of musicians. Step by step, they rose higher, as if ascending into the clouds. [64]

    俄顷之间，已到广陵矣。月色如昼，街陌绳直，寺观陈设之盛，灯火之光， 照灼台殿。士女华丽，若行化焉，而皆仰望曰：“仙人现于五色云中。”乃蹈舞 而拜，阗溢里巷。帝大悦焉，乃曰：“此真广陵也？”师曰：“请敕乐官奏《霓 裳羽衣》一曲，后可验矣。”于是作乐云中，瞻听之人，纷坛相蹈。曲终，帝意 将回，有顷之间，已到阙矣。帝极喜。
    　　
    　　人或谓仙师幻术造微，暂炫耳目。久之未决。后数旬，广陵奏云：“正月十 五日三更，有仙人乘彩云自西来，临孝感寺道场上，高数十丈。久之，又奏《霓 裳羽衣》一曲，曲终西去。官僚士女，无不具瞻。斯盖陛下孝诚感通，玄德昭著， 名应仙录，道冠帝图。不然，何以初元朝礼之晨而庆云现，小臣贱修之地而仙乐 陈。则垂衣裳者徒闻帝德，歌《南风》者才洽人心，岂与盛朝同日而语哉！”上 览表，大悦，方信师之不妄也。 [65]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [65]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 俄顷之间 - Means 'in a moment' or 'in a short while', indicating a brief period of time.
    • 已到广陵矣 - Means 'had already arrived at Guangling', with '矣' marking the completion of the action.
    • 月色如昼 - A vivid description meaning 'the moonlight was as bright as daylight'.
    • 街陌绳直 - Describes the streets as 'as straight as a string', emphasizing their neat and orderly layout.
    • 寺观陈设之盛 - Means 'the splendid arrangement of temples and observatories', with '盛' indicating grandeur.
    • 灯火之光，照灼台殿 - Describes the lights as 'shining brightly on the palaces and halls', with '照灼' emphasizing the intensity of the light.
    • 士女华丽，若行化焉 - Means 'the men and women were splendidly dressed, as if they were walking in a transformation', with '行化' suggesting a magical or divine appearance.
    • 皆仰望曰 - Means 'all looked up and said', with '皆' indicating everyone.
    • 仙人现于五色云中 - Means 'the immortal appeared in a cloud of five colors', with '五色云' referring to a colorful cloud often associated with immortals.
    • 乃蹈舞而拜 - Means 'they then danced and bowed', with '蹈舞' indicating dancing and '拜' indicating bowing.
    • 阗溢里巷 - Means 'the streets were filled with people', with '阗溢' indicating a crowded scene.
    • 帝大悦焉 - Means 'the emperor was greatly pleased', with '焉' indicating a sense of completion.
    • 此真广陵也 - Means 'this is truly Guangling', with '真' indicating authenticity.
    • 请敕乐官奏《霓裳羽衣》一曲 - Means 'please order the music officials to play the *Nirvana Robe* melody', with '敕' indicating an imperial command.
    • 后可验矣 - Means 'which can be verified later', with '验' indicating verification.
    • 于是作乐云中 - Means 'then the music was made in the clouds', with '云中' indicating the clouds.
    • 瞻听之人，纷坛相蹈 - Means 'the people who watched and listened were all over the place', with '纷坛相蹈' indicating a chaotic but excited scene.
    • 曲终，帝意将回 - Means 'when the music ended, the emperor's thoughts were about to return', with '将回' indicating a change in mood.
    • 有顷之间，已到阙矣 - Means 'in a short while, they had already arrived at the palace gates', with '阙' referring to the palace gates.
    • 帝极喜 - Means 'the emperor was extremely happy', with '极' indicating the highest degree.
    • 人或谓仙师幻术造微 - Means 'some people said the immortal teacher's magic was extremely subtle', with '造微' indicating subtlety.
    • 暂炫耳目 - Means 'temporarily dazzling the eyes and ears', with '炫' indicating dazzling.
    • 久之未决 - Means 'for a long time without a decision', with '久之' indicating a prolonged period.
    • 后数旬，广陵奏云 - Means 'after several weeks, Guangling reported', with '数旬' indicating several weeks.
    • 正月十五日三更 - Means 'on the 15th day of the first month at midnight', with '三更' indicating midnight.
    • 有仙人乘彩云自西来 - Means 'there was an immortal riding a colorful cloud coming from the west', with '乘彩云' indicating riding a colorful cloud.
    • 临孝感寺道场上 - Means 'arriving at the Daoist platform of Xianggan Temple', with '临' indicating arrival.
    • 高数十丈 - Means 'towering tens of feet high', with '数十丈' indicating a significant height.
    • 又奏《霓裳羽衣》一曲 - Means 'and played the *Nirvana Robe* melody again', with '又' indicating again.
    • 曲终西去 - Means 'when the music ended, the immortal went west', with '西去' indicating going west.
    • 官僚士女，无不具瞻 - Means 'officials, gentlemen, and ladies all looked up', with '无不' indicating all.
    • 斯盖陛下孝诚感通，玄德昭著 - Means 'this is because Your Majesty's filial piety and sincerity have moved the heavens, and Your Majesty's virtuous nature is clearly evident', with '感通' indicating moving the heavens and '昭著' indicating being clearly evident.
    • 名应仙录，道冠帝图 - Means 'Your Majesty's name is recorded in the immortal books, and Your Majesty's virtue surpasses that of emperors', with '仙录' indicating immortal books and '道冠帝图' indicating surpassing emperors.
    • 不然，何以初元朝礼之晨而庆云现 - Means 'otherwise, why would auspicious clouds appear in the morning of the first year's court ceremony', with '不然' indicating otherwise and '庆云' indicating auspicious clouds.
    • 小臣贱修之地而仙乐陈 - Means 'and why would immortal music be played in the humble abode of a lowly official', with '贱修之地' indicating a humble abode and '仙乐陈' indicating immortal music being played.
    • 则垂衣裳者徒闻帝德，歌《南风》者才洽人心 - Means 'those who wear robes only hear of Your Majesty's virtue, and those who sing the *South Wind* only touch people's hearts', with '垂衣裳者' indicating those who wear robes and '歌《南风》者' indicating those who sing the *South Wind*.
    • 岂与盛朝同日而语哉 - Means 'how could they be compared to a prosperous dynasty', with '岂与' indicating how could they be compared and '同日而语' indicating being compared.
    • 上览表，大悦 - Means 'the emperor read the report and was greatly pleased', with '览表' indicating reading the report.
    • 方信师之不妄也 - Means 'and then believed the teacher was not lying', with '方信' indicating then believing and '不妄' indicating not lying.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 广陵 - Refers to Guangling, an ancient city in present-day Yangzhou, Jiangsu Province, known for its cultural significance.
    • 五色云 - Refers to a colorful cloud often associated with immortals in Chinese mythology.
    • 霓裳羽衣 - Refers to the *Nirvana Robe* melody, a famous piece of music associated with the Tang Dynasty.
    • 孝感寺 - Refers to Xianggan Temple, a specific temple mentioned in the text.
    • 道场 - Refers to a Daoist platform or altar.
    • 初元朝礼之晨 - Refers to the morning of the first year's court ceremony, a significant event in the imperial calendar.
    • 庆云 - Refers to auspicious clouds, often seen as a sign of good fortune or divine favor.
    • 仙乐 - Refers to immortal music, often associated with divine or supernatural beings.
    • 垂衣裳者 - Refers to those who wear robes, often used to describe officials or dignitaries.
    • 歌《南风》者 - Refers to those who sing the *South Wind*, a reference to the *South Wind* song, which is often associated with harmony and prosperity.
    • 盛朝 - Refers to a prosperous dynasty, often used to describe the height of a civilization.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • 俄顷之间，已到广陵矣 - This sentence is a concise statement of arrival, with '俄顷之间' indicating a brief period and '已到广陵矣' indicating the completion of the action.
    • 月色如昼，街陌绳直，寺观陈设之盛，灯火之光，照灼台殿 - This series of descriptive clauses must be read together to form a vivid picture of the scene.
    • 士女华丽，若行化焉，而皆仰望曰 - This clause introduces the reaction of the people, with '士女华丽' describing their appearance and '若行化焉' suggesting a magical transformation.
    • 仙人现于五色云中 - This clause describes the appearance of the immortal, with '现于五色云中' indicating the setting.
    • 乃蹈舞而拜，阗溢里巷 - This clause describes the people's reaction, with '乃蹈舞而拜' indicating dancing and bowing and '阗溢里巷' indicating a crowded scene.
    • 帝大悦焉 - This clause describes the emperor's reaction, with '大悦焉' indicating his great pleasure.
    • 此真广陵也 - This clause is a rhetorical question affirming the authenticity of the place, with '此真广陵也' indicating that it is indeed Guangling.

    In a moment, they had already arrived at Guangling. The moonlight was as bright as daylight, the streets as straight as a string, and the splendid arrangement of temples and observatories, with the lights shining brightly on the palaces and halls. The men and women were splendidly dressed, as if they were walking in a transformation, and all looked up and said, "The immortal appeared in a cloud of five colors." They then danced and bowed, and the streets were filled with people. The emperor was greatly pleased, and he said, "This is truly Guangling?" The teacher replied, "Please order the music officials to play the Nirvana Robe melody, which can be verified later." Then the music was made in the clouds, and the people who watched and listened were all over the place. When the music ended, the emperor's thoughts were about to return, and in a short while, they had already arrived at the palace gates. The emperor was extremely happy.

    Some people said the immortal teacher's magic was extremely subtle, temporarily dazzling the eyes and ears. For a long time, there was no decision. After several weeks, Guangling reported, "On the 15th day of the first month at midnight, there was an immortal riding a colorful cloud coming from the west, arriving at the Daoist platform of Xianggan Temple, towering tens of feet high. After a while, they played the Nirvana Robe melody again, and when the music ended, the immortal went west. Officials, gentlemen, and ladies all looked up. This is because Your Majesty's filial piety and sincerity have moved the heavens, and Your Majesty's virtuous nature is clearly evident. Your Majesty's name is recorded in the immortal books, and Your Majesty's virtue surpasses that of emperors. Otherwise, why would auspicious clouds appear in the morning of the first year's court ceremony, and why would immortal music be played in the humble abode of a lowly official? Those who wear robes only hear of Your Majesty's virtue, and those who sing the South Wind only touch people's hearts. How could they be compared to a prosperous dynasty?" The emperor read the report and was greatly pleased, and then believed the teacher was not lying. [65]

    ○袁洪儿夸郎
    　　
    　　陈朱崖太守袁洪儿，小名夸郎，年二十，生来性好书，乐静，别处一院，颇 能玄言。尝野见翠翠鸟，命罗得之。袁甚好玩，清夜月明，彻烛长吟：“露湿寒 塘草，月映清淮流。”忽失翠鸟所在，见一双鬟婢子立在其左，曰：“袁郎此篇 甚为佳妙，然未知我二十七郎封郎能押剧韵，人为三言四言句诗，一句开口，一 句合咏。春诗曰：‘花落也，蛱蝶舞，人何多疾，吁足忧苦。’如剧韵押法之者， 有一二百首，不能尽记得。 [66]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [66]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 颇能玄言 - Means 'is quite capable of speaking in a profound or mystical manner.'
    • 未知我二十七郎封郎能押剧韵 - Means 'does not know if my 27th son, Feng Lang, can handle the complex rhyming.'
    • 人为三言四言句诗 - Means 'people write three-character and four-character poetic lines.'
    • 一句开口，一句合咏 - Means 'one line starts, one line ends.'
    • 春诗曰 - Means 'the spring poem says.'
    • 花落也，蛱蝶舞，人何多疾，吁足忧苦 - A four-character poetic line that translates to 'Flowers fall, butterflies dance, why do people suffer so much, it's enough to cause worry and distress.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 陈朱崖太守 - Refers to the governor of Zhu崖 (a region in present-day Hainan, China) during the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
    • 翠翠鸟 - A type of bird, often associated with beauty and grace in Chinese literature.
    • 清夜月明 - A common theme in Chinese poetry, describing a clear night with bright moonlight.
    • 彻烛长吟 - Means 'lit candles and recited poetry,' indicating a solitary and contemplative moment.
    • 二十七郎封郎 - Refers to the 27th son of the governor, named Feng Lang, who is likely a young man with literary talent.

    Proper Nouns

    • 袁洪儿 - A proper name, likely a historical or literary figure.
    • 夸郎 - A nickname or pet name, which is not a standard term and requires explanation.
    • 陈朱崖太守 - A title, referring to the governor of Zhu崖.
    • 翠翠鸟 - A type of bird, often associated with beauty and grace in Chinese literature.
    • 二十七郎封郎 - A proper name, referring to the 27th son of the governor, named Feng Lang.

    Governor Yuan Hong'er of Zhu Ya, nicknamed Kuanglang, was twenty years old. From birth, he had a fondness for books and enjoyed a quiet life. He lived in a separate courtyard and was quite capable of speaking in a profound manner. Once, while out in the wild, he saw a green bird and ordered it to be captured. Yuan greatly enjoyed this bird. On a clear night under the bright moon, he lit candles and recited poetry: "Dew wets the grass in the cold pond, the moon reflects on the clear river." Suddenly, he lost track of where the green bird was. He saw a pair of maidens with braided hair standing to his left and said, "This piece by Master Yuan is very excellent, but I wonder if my 27th son, Feng Lang, can handle the complex rhyming.
